satire_attempt: Behold, the wisdom of the ancient sages, repackaged for the modern staff engineer: deploy the migration, but release attachment to whether the migration deploys. Push to main with the equanimity of one who has read the Gita and the Datadog quickstart. The truly enlightened engineer ships, then closes the laptop and meditates upon the dashboard from a place of pure non-doership, free from the binding karma of error rates. Brought to you by Mindful Engineering Quarterly — $5,000 per retreat, complimentary Stoic enamel mug.
verdict_reason: The satire only lands if I delete (1) the falsifiability clause that explicitly forbids the 'stop reading dashboards' reading, (2) the second concrete scenario where rewriting-an-internal-CLI-no-one-asked-for is named as comfort-displacement, and (3) the v3.1 'Third' clause: 'inaction disguised as caution, and it is rejected here just as firmly as the grasping for fruits.' All three pre-empt the cope reading. Mocking the verse on its own terms requires inventing claims it does not make — specifically, the LinkedIn 'detach from metrics' reading the verse explicitly forbids. PASS.
